info.headline: Flood Watch for Warburton River
info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.headline: Flood Watch for Cooper Creek
info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.headline: Flood Watch for Simpson Desert
info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.headline: Flood Watch for Eastern Great Victoria Desert
info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.

info.description:
FLOODING POSSIBLE IN PARTS OF THE NORTH AND NORTH-WEST CATCHMENTS FROM MONDAY
A tropical low situated in northern WA is feeding rich tropical moisture along a surface trough to its southeast. An upper trough is moving through southern WA. The combination of these two systems will bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northwestern parts of the state from late this evening. Heavy rainfall is expected to slowly move eastwards on Monday.
Catchments in the Flood Watch area are moderately wet and are likely to respond quickly to this rainfall.
Forecast rainfall may cause river and creek level rises, prolonged overland flooding, and ponding from Monday onwards. Roads access may be affected, and some communities and homesteads my become isolated. Check road conditions before travelling.
info.instruction: * Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. * Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. * Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. * Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed. * For local emergency management warnings and advice visit www.ses.sa.gov.au/incidents-and-warnings/incidents-and-warnings-map/.
